ai-fluency-for-nonprofits_10.q: Which loop helps you make thoughtful, responsible choices about when and how to use AI?

claude-in-amazon-bedrock_14.q: You're building a chat app and users complain that responses take too long to appear. What feature should you implement?
opts: Use streaming to show text as it's generated

claude-in-amazon-bedrock_22.q: You need test data for evaluating your prompt. You want to create realistic examples quickly without writing them all by hand. What's the best approach?
opts: Use the same example over and over
Use Claude to automatically generate test cases
Ask your friends to write them

claude-in-amazon-bedrock_29.q: You want an AI to write movie reviews in a specific style. What's the best way to show the AI exactly what you want?
opts: Tell it to copy famous movie critics
Describe the style in great detail
Give it a sample movie review as an example

claude-in-amazon-bedrock_42.q: When using tools, what happens right after Claude asks for specific external data?
opts: Claude analyzes the original question again
The user needs to approve the data request
Your server runs code to fetch the requested information
Claude provides the final answer immediately
A technique that adds context to document chunks before storing them to improve search accuracy
A way to reduce the size of document chunks
A system for automatically generating new content from existing documents
A method for searching through documents faster

claude-in-amazon-bedrock_60.q: You send Claude the same long document twice in a row. What does prompt caching help with?
opts: It stores your conversation history permanently
It reduces the document's file size
It saves the computational work from processing the text in the document

claude-in-amazon-bedrock_72.q: You're building a chat app where users ask Claude about their GitHub data. Without MCP, what's the main problem you'd face?
opts: Claude can't connect to the internet
You'd have to write and maintain all the GitHub tool functions yourself

claude-in-amazon-bedrock_82.q: What are tokens in the context of language models?
opts: The smallest units a language model can understand

claude-with-google-vertex_11.q: How happy are you with this course so far?

claude-with-google-vertex_16.q: You're building a chat app that talks to Claude. Where should you put your API key?

claude-with-google-vertex_24.q: You've learned techniques for writing better prompts, but now you want to measure how well they actually work. What do you need?

claude-with-google-vertex_31.q: You're giving your AI a long document to analyze along with your instructions. What would help the AI understand your prompt better?
opts: Put everything in one big paragraph
Write the document in all capital letters
Separate sections with lots of blank lines

claude-with-google-vertex_45.q: When Claude wants to use a tool, it sends back a response that's different from usual. What does this response contain?
opts: Both text blocks and tool use blocks
Only tool requests with no text

claude-with-google-vertex_55.q: You're setting up a system to handle large documents. Instead of using everything at once, you break documents into smaller pieces and search for relevant ones. What is this approach called?

claude-with-google-vertex_63.q: When Extended Thinking is enabled, what two parts will Claude's response contain?
opts: A summary block and a detail block
A thinking block and a text block
A draft block and a final block
A question block and an answer block

claude-with-google-vertex_75.q: User-controlled workflows that are triggered through UI interactions like button clicks or slash commands. This definition describes which MCP primitive?

claude-with-google-vertex_91.q: You want Claude to analyze a product image for 6 different materials at once. Instead of one huge prompt, what should you do?
opts: Write a longer, more detailed prompt
Send 6 separate requests in parallel, then combine results
Ask the user to pick one material first
Use an agent with material tools

claude-with-google-vertex_92.q: You're using Claude to brainstorm creative story ideas and want more variety in the suggestions. What should you do?
opts: Send the same request multiple times

introduction-to-claude-cowork_11.q: What do subagents enable when Cowork is handling a large task with independent pieces?
opts: They let Cowork run tasks on other computers in your network
They automatically retry failed steps without telling you
They work on separate pieces at the same time, each with its own focused context
They store each piece of work permanently so it can be reused later

model-context-protocol-advanced-topics_1.q: Your MCP server needs to use Claude to summarize data, but you don't want the server to handle API costs. What feature should you use?
